pages.hextra.hextra_vectors.title: Hextra Vectors
hextra.page.vectors.one: The left-hand counter-clockwise pattern adds (1, 1, 1) to the stack; the right-hand clockwise pattern adds (-1, -1, -1).
hextra.page.vectors.greater: If the first vector is longer than the second, return True. Otherwise, return False.
hextra.page.vectors.less: If the first vector is shorter than the second, return True. Otherwise, return False.
hextra.page.vectors.greater_eq: If the first vector is longer or the same length as the second, return True. Otherwise, return False.
hextra.page.vectors.less_eq: If the first vector is shorter or the same length the second, return True. Otherwise, return False.
pages.hextra.hextra_vec_literals.title: Vector Literals
hextra.page.vec_literals.1: Interestingly, flipping any $(action)Vector Reflection/$ horizontally allows it to be subject to the same rules as $(l:patterns/numbers)$(action)Number Reflection/$. The direction of the head determines the direction, much like the tail of Vector Reflection.
hextra.page.vec_literals.2: A head that sharply bends to the left will output a vector along the $(l:patterns/consts#hexcasting:const/vec/x)$(action)X axis/$.
hextra.page.vec_literals.3: A head that doesn't bend will output a vector along the $(l:patterns/consts#hexcasting:const/vec/y)$(action)Y axis/$.
hextra.page.vec_literals.4: A head that sharply bends to the right will output a vector along the $(l:patterns/consts#hexcasting:const/vec/z)$(action)Z axis/$.
hextra.page.vec_literals.5: A head that slightly bends to the left will output a vector where $(l:patterns/hextra_vectors#hextra:haha_ha_one)$(action)all 3 components are the same/$.
hextra.page.vec_literals.6: First, I draw one of the eight shapes shown on the prior pages. Next, the $(italic)angles/$ following will modify a running count starting at 0.$(li)Forward: Add 1$(li)Left: Add 5$(li)Right: Add 10$(li)Sharp Left: Multiply by 2$(li)Sharp Right: Divide by 2.$(br)The clockwise version of the pattern, on the right of the other page, will negate the value at the very end. (The left-hand counter-clockwise version keeps the number positive).$(p)Once I finish drawing, the a vector's pushed to the top of the stack.
hextra.page.vec_literals.example.y7.head: Example 2
